explanations.1: This line tells Docker to use a newer syntax version that enables advanced caching features used later in the file.
explanations.24: The --mount flags create temporary storage caches during this build step so apt package downloads don't have to be repeated if you rebuild the image.
explanations.56: Similar to line 24, this caches downloaded files for the yq tool installation to speed up rebuilds.
explanations.71: This caches pip's package downloads so Python dependencies don't have to be re-downloaded on future rebuilds.
explanations.85: This caches npm package downloads in the user's home directory to speed up Node.js package installation on rebuilds.
explanations.108: The ${USER_UID:-1000} syntax means 'use the value of USER_UID if it's set, otherwise default to 1000' — it's a fallback pattern.
explanations.124: This caches Quarto's downloaded tools directory so the Chromium installation doesn't need to be repeated on rebuilds.
explanations.136: The ${LOCAL_WORKSPACE} variable here isn't defined in this Dockerfile — it's expected to be passed in from the host environment when the container runs.
